market_context: U.S. futures were weaker ahead of the open, with Nasdaq 100 futures down about 0.6% to 1.35% and S&P 500 futures modestly lower, while Dow futures were slightly positive in one feed. Recent news flow points to higher oil, weaker semiconductor sentiment, and a firmer 10-year yield near 4.56%, which is a mix that typically pressures growth-heavy indexes more than value stocks.[1][2][4]

predictions.reasoning: SPY is starting from a prior close of 747.71, while futures are pointing to a soft open and the 10-year yield remains elevated near 4.56%. Benzinga reports S&P 500 futures lower and notes weakness in tech and broader index performance, which supports a mild downside bias for the session.[2][4]

QQQ is the most exposed to the overnight weakness in Nasdaq 100 futures, which were down between 0.62% and 1.35% in the cited feeds.[1][4] With chip and AI sentiment under pressure and rates still high, I expect QQQ to underperform the broad market again today.[2][8]

NVDA is likely to stay under pressure because the overnight move in Nasdaq futures was led by weakness in memory-chip and AI-related stocks.[8][4] A softer risk tone and higher-rate backdrop typically compress valuation-sensitive semiconductors more than the market average.[2]

TSLA tends to trade as a high-beta growth name, so a weaker Nasdaq open and rising risk aversion are negative setup factors.[1][4] The move does not need to be large for the stock to finish below the prior close if the index weakness persists through midday.[2]

DIA has the best relative setup among the major ETFs because Dow futures were slightly positive in one feed while Nasdaq futures were clearly weaker, implying a possible rotation away from growth into large-cap industrial/value exposure.[1][4] The recent 5-day trend is also stronger for DIA than QQQ, which supports mild relative outperformance even if the broader tape is choppy.[baseline data]
